Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022. Sendero cuatro encantos. After setting up nets by Phaethornis lek. With Salma. Stopped along trail for breakfast at 930. Walking very slow, doing playback for various species. Recording. Joined at end by Diego, Valentina, Mori, and Callito (Diego had his own list running). Started at nets, walked up trail, ended count back at nets.

Quebrada El Terco. Delete all T. Florida and change to t johannae ---Woke at 430 and started walking at 5. Hit the high tide and had to ford the first river then swim my stuff across the second river close to termales in two trips. Got to the hospedaje where GuereGuere gave us a tip on the Bucco. Walked up the stream to the cascada and back. Good flock where we had the C. berlepschi yesterday.--Very beautiful morning, sun shining, fluffy clouds overhead, perfect temperature. --Started walking back around 830 making it the Vijo at 910. Finished packing and we pushed Carlos’ lancha in the sea and took off for Jurbida

Tayra!--Walked down beach at 530 and went back to the thermal baths. Found a trail on the backside of the termales that looked good. Turned out to be the path to Cuatro Encantos. Very wide and well worn. Decent activity for the choco with a few flocks. Particularly productive was a chagra about 1/4 of the away along the trail with lots of birds coming to the edge
Guillermo Gomez Fernandez, Maria Teresa Jaramillo, Luis Fernando Serna, Jin

Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022. El Amargal: camino hasta cuchilla de Arusí. With Armancio as guide. Light rain and super quiet overall. After walking up the trail a few hundred meters we turned back and I ended the list a little short of the El Amargal buildings when it was getting dark.

Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022. Walked east from the town of Termales, reached the "termales spa del pacifico", and then took the Camino Cuatro Encantos. We walked only a few hundred meters along the camino and then turned back. Scouting for Manacus. With Sebastian, Maira, and guides. Ebird track for this list is WAY off due to user error--accidentally continued a list from a previous site. Have since fixed error, except for lingering eBird track.

Walked around some shitty cattle pastures and trails with local guide, Robert Taylor. Was a little peeved at the shitty habitat he brought me too. We bush whacked our way to the mangroves. I got separated from him for a bit and when I refound him, he was smoking a joint made with a dried banana leaf. We shared the joint and reflected on how special the mangroves and forest are.
Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022. After setting up nets. With Salma, Mori, and Callito.

Valeria and Glenn woke at 4 and hiked down beach to meet Andre at Termales. We then walked down path towards Playa Terco and met our guide Guere Guere at his house. A colorful older gentleman from Bogota and former world traveler living a bit of a retired pirate’s life. Walked down beach and took a side path to reach Quebrada El Terco. A gentle, sandy stream through excellent forest. Shallow enough for boots for the most part. Where the valley narrowed we took a side trail up to a ridge that connected with the trails of El Cantil lodge and explored a bit up there. We returned to the steam around 10 and walked a short ways further up the steam to a impressive cascade with a small pool for swimming. Guere Guere went in in his birthday suit, Andre, Valeria, y yo were a bit more modest. Lots of rain on walk back, got completely soaked.

A gentle, sandy stream through excellent forest. Shallow enough for boots for the most part. Where the valley narrowed we took a side trail up to a ridge that connected with the trails of El Cantil lodge and explored a bit up there. We returned to the steam around 10 and walked a short ways further up the steam to a impressive cascade with a small pool for swimming. Guere Guere went in in his birthday suit, Andre, Valeria, y yo were a bit more modest. Lots of rain on walk back, got completely soaked.

Tayra!--Walked down beach at 530 and went back to the thermal baths. Found a trail on the backside of the termales that looked good. Turned out to be the path to Cuatro Encantos. Very wide and well worn. Decent activity for the choco with a few flocks. Particularly productive was a chagra about 1/4 of the away along the trail with lots of birds coming to the edge

Quebrada El Terco. Delete all T. Florida and change to t johannae ---Woke at 430 and started walking at 5. Hit the high tide and had to ford the first river then swim my stuff across the second river close to termales in two trips. Got to the hospedaje where GuereGuere gave us a tip on the Bucco. Walked up the stream to the cascada and back. Good flock where we had the C. berlepschi yesterday.--Very beautiful morning, sun shining, fluffy clouds overhead, perfect temperature. --Started walking back around 830 making it the Vijo at 910. Finished packing and we pushed Carlos’ lancha in the sea and took off for Jurbida

En el lugar confluyen varios ecosistemas, es una punta que se une con un islote cubierto de bosque, percha de cientos de aves que cruzan. Allí desemboca el río Terquito bordeado de selva tropical. Adicionalmente al existir una vivienda a orillas del río hay jardines, frutales y pequeños cultivos. A lo largo de la playa se conserva el bosque húmedo tropical y detrás se eleva la serranía. El cruce de aves marinas y terrestres es contínuo allí.

Valeria and Glenn woke at 4 and hiked down beach to meet Andre at Termales. We then walked down path towards Playa Terco and met our guide Guere Guere at his house. A colorful older gentleman from Bogota and former world traveler living a bit of a retired pirate’s life. Walked down beach and took a side path to reach Quebrada El Terco. A gentle, sandy stream through excellent forest. Shallow enough for boots for the most part. Where the valley narrowed we took a side trail up to a ridge that connected with the trails of El Cantil lodge and explored a bit up there. We returned to the steam around 10 and walked a short ways further up the steam to a impressive cascade with a small pool for swimming. Guere Guere went in in his birthday suit, Andre, Valeria, y yo were a bit more modest. Lots of rain on walk back, got completely soaked.

Quebrada El Terco. Delete all T. Florida and change to t johannae ---Woke at 430 and started walking at 5. Hit the high tide and had to ford the first river then swim my stuff across the second river close to termales in two trips. Got to the hospedaje where GuereGuere gave us a tip on the Bucco. Walked up the stream to the cascada and back. Good flock where we had the C. berlepschi yesterday.--Very beautiful morning, sun shining, fluffy clouds overhead, perfect temperature. --Started walking back around 830 making it the Vijo at 910. Finished packing and we pushed Carlos’ lancha in the sea and took off for Jurbida

Valeria and Glenn woke at 4 and hiked down beach to meet Andre at Termales. We then walked down path towards Playa Terco and met our guide Guere Guere at his house. A colorful older gentleman from Bogota and former world traveler living a bit of a retired pirate’s life. Walked down beach and took a side path to reach Quebrada El Terco. A gentle, sandy stream through excellent forest. Shallow enough for boots for the most part. Where the valley narrowed we took a side trail up to a ridge that connected with the trails of El Cantil lodge and explored a bit up there. We returned to the steam around 10 and walked a short ways further up the steam to a impressive cascade with a small pool for swimming. Guere Guere went in in his birthday suit, Andre, Valeria, y yo were a bit more modest. Lots of rain on walk back, got completely soaked.

Quebrada El Terco. Delete all T. Florida and change to t johannae ---Woke at 430 and started walking at 5. Hit the high tide and had to ford the first river then swim my stuff across the second river close to termales in two trips. Got to the hospedaje where GuereGuere gave us a tip on the Bucco. Walked up the stream to the cascada and back. Good flock where we had the C. berlepschi yesterday.--Very beautiful morning, sun shining, fluffy clouds overhead, perfect temperature. --Started walking back around 830 making it the Vijo at 910. Finished packing and we pushed Carlos’ lancha in the sea and took off for Jurbida

A colorful older gentleman from Bogota and former world traveler living a bit of a retired pirate’s life. Walked down beach and took a side path to reach Quebrada El Terco. A gentle, sandy stream through excellent forest. Shallow enough for boots for the most part. Where the valley narrowed we took a side trail up to a ridge that connected with the trails of El Cantil lodge and explored a bit up there. We returned to the steam around 10 and walked a short ways further up the steam to a impressive cascade with a small pool for swimming. Guere Guere went in in his birthday suit, Andre, Valeria, y yo were a bit more modest. Lots of rain on walk back, got completely soaked.

En el lugar confluyen varios ecosistemas, es una punta que se une con un islote cubierto de bosque, percha de cientos de aves que cruzan. Allí desemboca el río Terquito bordeado de selva tropical. Adicionalmente al existir una vivienda a orillas del río hay jardines, frutales y pequeños cultivos. A lo largo de la playa se conserva el bosque húmedo tropical y detrás se eleva la serranía. El cruce de aves marinas y terrestres es contínuo allí.

Valeria and Glenn woke at 4 and hiked down beach to meet Andre at Termales. We then walked down path towards Playa Terco and met our guide Guere Guere at his house. A colorful older gentleman from Bogota and former world traveler living a bit of a retired pirate’s life. Walked down beach and took a side path to reach Quebrada El Terco. A gentle, sandy stream through excellent forest. Shallow enough for boots for the most part. Where the valley narrowed we took a side trail up to a ridge that connected with the trails of El Cantil lodge and explored a bit up there. We returned to the steam around 10 and walked a short ways further up the steam to a impressive cascade with a small pool for swimming. Guere Guere went in in his birthday suit, Andre, Valeria, y yo were a bit more modest. Lots of rain on walk back, got completely soaked.

Quebrada El Terco. Delete all T. Florida and change to t johannae ---Woke at 430 and started walking at 5. Hit the high tide and had to ford the first river then swim my stuff across the second river close to termales in two trips. Got to the hospedaje where GuereGuere gave us a tip on the Bucco. Walked up the stream to the cascada and back. Good flock where we had the C. berlepschi yesterday.--Very beautiful morning, sun shining, fluffy clouds overhead, perfect temperature. --Started walking back around 830 making it the Vijo at 910. Finished packing and we pushed Carlos’ lancha in the sea and took off for Jurbida

Quebrada El Terco. Delete all T. Florida and change to t johannae ---Woke at 430 and started walking at 5. Hit the high tide and had to ford the first river then swim my stuff across the second river close to termales in two trips. Got to the hospedaje where GuereGuere gave us a tip on the Bucco. Walked up the stream to the cascada and back. Good flock where we had the C. berlepschi yesterday.--Very beautiful morning, sun shining, fluffy clouds overhead, perfect temperature. --Started walking back around 830 making it the Vijo at 910. Finished packing and we pushed Carlos’ lancha in the sea and took off for Jurbida

Valeria and Glenn woke at 4 and hiked down beach to meet Andre at Termales. We then walked down path towards Playa Terco and met our guide Guere Guere at his house. A colorful older gentleman from Bogota and former world traveler living a bit of a retired pirate’s life. Walked down beach and took a side path to reach Quebrada El Terco. A gentle, sandy stream through excellent forest. Shallow enough for boots for the most part. Where the valley narrowed we took a side trail up to a ridge that connected with the trails of El Cantil lodge and explored a bit up there. We returned to the steam around 10 and walked a short ways further up the steam to a impressive cascade with a small pool for swimming. Guere Guere went in in his birthday suit, Andre, Valeria, y yo were a bit more modest. Lots of rain on walk back, got completely soaked.

Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022. With Armancio as guide, we started at the El Amargal clearing then walked the Camino hasta Cuchilla de Arusí. Ending at clearing near the cuchilla (ridgetop) where we ate breakfast and hung out for a while. Good lighting and open views there for some canopy birds.

Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022. Sendero cuatro encantos. After setting up nets by Phaethornis lek. With Salma. Stopped along trail for breakfast at 930. Walking very slow, doing playback for various species. Recording. Joined at end by Diego, Valentina, Mori, and Callito (Diego had his own list running). Started at nets, walked up trail, ended count back at nets.

Tayra!--Walked down beach at 530 and went back to the thermal baths. Found a trail on the backside of the termales that looked good. Turned out to be the path to Cuatro Encantos. Very wide and well worn. Decent activity for the choco with a few flocks. Particularly productive was a chagra about 1/4 of the away along the trail with lots of birds coming to the edge

Valeria and Glenn woke at 4 and hiked down beach to meet Andre at Termales. We then walked down path towards Playa Terco and met our guide Guere Guere at his house. A colorful older gentleman from Bogota and former world traveler living a bit of a retired pirate’s life. Walked down beach and took a side path to reach Quebrada El Terco. A gentle, sandy stream through excellent forest. Shallow enough for boots for the most part. Where the valley narrowed we took a side trail up to a ridge that connected with the trails of El Cantil lodge and explored a bit up there. We returned to the steam around 10 and walked a short ways further up the steam to a impressive cascade with a small pool for swimming. Guere Guere went in in his birthday suit, Andre, Valeria, y yo were a bit more modest. Lots of rain on walk back, got completely soaked.

En el lugar confluyen varios ecosistemas, es una punta que se une con un islote cubierto de bosque, percha de cientos de aves que cruzan. Allí desemboca el río Terquito bordeado de selva tropical. Adicionalmente al existir una vivienda a orillas del río hay jardines, frutales y pequeños cultivos. A lo largo de la playa se conserva el bosque húmedo tropical y detrás se eleva la serranía. El cruce de aves marinas y terrestres es contínuo allí.

Quebrada El Terco. Delete all T. Florida and change to t johannae ---Woke at 430 and started walking at 5. Hit the high tide and had to ford the first river then swim my stuff across the second river close to termales in two trips. Got to the hospedaje where GuereGuere gave us a tip on the Bucco. Walked up the stream to the cascada and back. Good flock where we had the C. berlepschi yesterday.--Very beautiful morning, sun shining, fluffy clouds overhead, perfect temperature. --Started walking back around 830 making it the Vijo at 910. Finished packing and we pushed Carlos’ lancha in the sea and took off for Jurbida

Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022. Sendero cuatro encantos. After setting up nets by Phaethornis lek. With Salma. Stopped along trail for breakfast at 930. Walking very slow, doing playback for various species. Recording. Joined at end by Diego, Valentina, Mori, and Callito (Diego had his own list running). Started at nets, walked up trail, ended count back at nets.

Valeria and Glenn woke at 4 and hiked down beach to meet Andre at Termales. We then walked down path towards Playa Terco and met our guide Guere Guere at his house. A colorful older gentleman from Bogota and former world traveler living a bit of a retired pirate’s life. Walked down beach and took a side path to reach Quebrada El Terco. A gentle, sandy stream through excellent forest. Shallow enough for boots for the most part. Where the valley narrowed we took a side trail up to a ridge that connected with the trails of El Cantil lodge and explored a bit up there. We returned to the steam around 10 and walked a short ways further up the steam to a impressive cascade with a small pool for swimming. Guere Guere went in in his birthday suit, Andre, Valeria, y yo were a bit more modest. Lots of rain on walk back, got completely soaked.

Quebrada El Terco. Delete all T. Florida and change to t johannae ---Woke at 430 and started walking at 5. Hit the high tide and had to ford the first river then swim my stuff across the second river close to termales in two trips. Got to the hospedaje where GuereGuere gave us a tip on the Bucco. Walked up the stream to the cascada and back. Good flock where we had the C. berlepschi yesterday.--Very beautiful morning, sun shining, fluffy clouds overhead, perfect temperature. --Started walking back around 830 making it the Vijo at 910. Finished packing and we pushed Carlos’ lancha in the sea and took off for Jurbida

Walked around some shitty cattle pastures and trails with local guide, Robert Taylor. Was a little peeved at the shitty habitat he brought me too. We bush whacked our way to the mangroves. Quebrada El Terco. Delete all T. Florida and change to t johannae ---Woke at 430 and started walking at 5. Hit the high tide and had to ford the first river then swim my stuff across the second river close to termales in two trips. Got to the hospedaje where GuereGuere gave us a tip on the Bucco. Walked up the stream to the cascada and back. Good flock where we had the C. berlepschi yesterday.--Very beautiful morning, sun shining, fluffy clouds overhead, perfect temperature. --Started walking back around 830 making it the Vijo at 910. Finished packing and we pushed Carlos’ lancha in the sea and took off for Jurbida

En el lugar confluyen varios ecosistemas, es una punta que se une con un islote cubierto de bosque, percha de cientos de aves que cruzan. Allí desemboca el río Terquito bordeado de selva tropical. Adicionalmente al existir una vivienda a orillas del río hay jardines, frutales y pequeños cultivos. A lo largo de la playa se conserva el bosque húmedo tropical y detrás se eleva la serranía. El cruce de aves marinas y terrestres es contínuo allí.

Valeria and Glenn woke at 4 and hiked down beach to meet Andre at Termales. We then walked down path towards Playa Terco and met our guide Guere Guere at his house. A colorful older gentleman from Bogota and former world traveler living a bit of a retired pirate’s life. Walked down beach and took a side path to reach Quebrada El Terco. A gentle, sandy stream through excellent forest. Shallow enough for boots for the most part. Where the valley narrowed we took a side trail up to a ridge that connected with the trails of El Cantil lodge and explored a bit up there. We returned to the steam around 10 and walked a short ways further up the steam to a impressive cascade with a small pool for swimming. Guere Guere went in in his birthday suit, Andre, Valeria, y yo were a bit more modest. Lots of rain on walk back, got completely soaked.

A colorful older gentleman from Bogota [AEM edit: from Medellin I think] and former world traveler living a bit of a retired pirate’s life. Walked down beach and took a side path to reach Quebrada El Terco. A gentle, sandy stream through excellent forest. Shallow enough for boots for the most part. Where the valley narrowed we took a side trail up to a ridge that connected with the trails of El Cantil lodge and explored a bit up there. We returned to the steam around 10 and walked a short ways further up the stream to an impressive cascade with a small pool for swimming. Guere Guere went in his birthday suit; Andre, Valeria, y yo were a bit more modest.

Quebrada El Terco. Delete all T. Florida and change to t johannae ---Woke at 430 and started walking at 5. Hit the high tide and had to ford the first river then swim my stuff across the second river close to termales in two trips. Got to the hospedaje where GuereGuere gave us a tip on the Bucco. Walked up the stream to the cascada and back. Good flock where we had the C. berlepschi yesterday.--Very beautiful morning, sun shining, fluffy clouds overhead, perfect temperature. --Started walking back around 830 making it the Vijo at 910. Finished packing and we pushed Carlos’ lancha in the sea and took off for Jurbida

En el lugar confluyen varios ecosistemas, es una punta que se une con un islote cubierto de bosque, percha de cientos de aves que cruzan. Allí desemboca el río Terquito bordeado de selva tropical. Adicionalmente al existir una vivienda a orillas del río hay jardines, frutales y pequeños cultivos. A lo largo de la playa se conserva el bosque húmedo tropical y detrás se eleva la serranía. El cruce de aves marinas y terrestres es contínuo allí.

Valeria and Glenn woke at 4 and hiked down beach to meet Andre at Termales. We then walked down path towards Playa Terco and met our guide Guere Guere at his house. A colorful older gentleman from Bogota and former world traveler living a bit of a retired pirate’s life. Walked down beach and took a side path to reach Quebrada El Terco. A gentle, sandy stream through excellent forest. Shallow enough for boots for the most part. Where the valley narrowed we took a side trail up to a ridge that connected with the trails of El Cantil lodge and explored a bit up there. We returned to the steam around 10 and walked a short ways further up the steam to a impressive cascade with a small pool for swimming. Guere Guere went in in his birthday suit, Andre, Valeria, y yo were a bit more modest. Lots of rain on walk back, got completely soaked.

Tayra!--Walked down beach at 530 and went back to the thermal baths. Found a trail on the backside of the termales that looked good. Turned out to be the path to Cuatro Encantos. En el lugar confluyen varios ecosistemas, es una punta que se une con un islote cubierto de bosque, percha de cientos de aves que cruzan. Allí desemboca el río Terquito bordeado de selva tropical. Adicionalmente al existir una vivienda a orillas del río hay jardines, frutales y pequeños cultivos. A lo largo de la playa se conserva el bosque húmedo tropical y detrás se eleva la serranía. El cruce de aves marinas y terrestres es contínuo allí.

Quebrada El Terco. Delete all T. Florida and change to t johannae ---Woke at 430 and started walking at 5. Hit the high tide and had to ford the first river then swim my stuff across the second river close to termales in two trips. Got to the hospedaje where GuereGuere gave us a tip on the Bucco. Walked up the stream to the cascada and back. Good flock where we had the C. berlepschi yesterday.--Very beautiful morning, sun shining, fluffy clouds overhead, perfect temperature. --Started walking back around 830 making it the Vijo at 910. Finished packing and we pushed Carlos’ lancha in the sea and took off for Jurbida

En el lugar confluyen varios ecosistemas, es una punta que se une con un islote cubierto de bosque, percha de cientos de aves que cruzan. Allí desemboca el río Terquito bordeado de selva tropical. Adicionalmente al existir una vivienda a orillas del río hay jardines, frutales y pequeños cultivos. A lo largo de la playa se conserva el bosque húmedo tropical y detrás se eleva la serranía. El cruce de aves marinas y terrestres es contínuo allí.

En el lugar confluyen varios ecosistemas, es una punta que se une con un islote cubierto de bosque, percha de cientos de aves que cruzan. Allí desemboca el río Terquito bordeado de selva tropical. Adicionalmente al existir una vivienda a orillas del río hay jardines, frutales y pequeños cultivos. A lo largo de la playa se conserva el bosque húmedo tropical y detrás se eleva la serranía. El cruce de aves marinas y terrestres es contínuo allí.

Ebird track for this list is WAY off due to user error--accidentally continued a list from a previous site. Have since fixed error, except for lingering eBird track.
Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022. With Diego Cueva, Valentina Nieto, Mori, Salma, and Callito. From town to early section of the camino cuatro encantos.
Manacus2022_Manacus expedition LSU-Humboldt Institute 2022. With Diego Cueva, Valentina Nieto, Mori, Salma, and Callito. From town to early section of the camino cuatro encantos.

Tayra!--Walked down beach at 530 and went back to the thermal baths. Found a trail on the backside of the termales that looked good. Turned out to be the path to Cuatro Encantos. Very wide and well worn. Decent activity for the choco with a few flocks. Particularly productive was a chagra about 1/4 of the away along the trail with lots of birds coming to the edge

Quebrada El Terco. Delete all T. Florida and change to t johannae ---Woke at 430 and started walking at 5. Hit the high tide and had to ford the first river then swim my stuff across the second river close to termales in two trips. Got to the hospedaje where GuereGuere gave us a tip on the Bucco. Walked up the stream to the cascada and back. Good flock where we had the C. berlepschi yesterday.--Very beautiful morning, sun shining, fluffy clouds overhead, perfect temperature. --Started walking back around 830 making it the Vijo at 910. Finished packing and we pushed Carlos’ lancha in the sea and took off for Jurbida

Walked around some shitty cattle pastures and trails with local guide, Robert Taylor. Was a little peeved at the shitty habitat he brought me too. We bush whacked our way to the mangroves. I got separated from him for a bit and when I refound him, he was smoking a joint made with a dried banana leaf. We shared the joint and reflected on how special the mangroves and forest are.

Valeria and Glenn woke at 4 and hiked down beach to meet Andre at Termales. We then walked down path towards Playa Terco and met our guide Guere Guere at his house. A colorful older gentleman from Bogota and former world traveler living a bit of a retired pirate’s life. Walked down beach and took a side path to reach Quebrada El Terco. A gentle, sandy stream through excellent forest. Shallow enough for boots for the most part. Where the valley narrowed we took a side trail up to a ridge that connected with the trails of El Cantil lodge and explored a bit up there. We returned to the steam around 10 and walked a short ways further up the steam to a impressive cascade with a small pool for swimming. Guere Guere went in in his birthday suit, Andre, Valeria, y yo were a bit more modest. Lots of rain on walk back, got completely soaked.

Valeria and Glenn woke at 4 and hiked down beach to meet Andre at Termales. We then walked down path towards Playa Terco and met our guide Guere Guere at his house. A colorful older gentleman from Bogota and former world traveler living a bit of a retired pirate’s life. Walked down beach and took a side path to reach Quebrada El Terco. A gentle, sandy stream through excellent forest. Shallow enough for boots for the most part. Where the valley narrowed we took a side trail up to a ridge that connected with the trails of El Cantil lodge and explored a bit up there. We returned to the steam around 10 and walked a short ways further up the steam to a impressive cascade with a small pool for swimming. Guere Guere went in in his birthday suit, Andre, Valeria, y yo were a bit more modest. Lots of rain on walk back, got completely soaked.
